desc:
The ground and grass of the field is damp and shifts a bit when walked upon. The
ridge here continues from the east side curving around to the south side while
the plains stretch northwards and slope downwards in the west.
name: Damp Plain

desc:
The wide-stretched grassy fields end with a vertical, unclimbable cliff wall to
the north. Many yards above, the plateau continues northwards with even more
plains. To the east the terrain becomes rockier and two enormous boulders can be
seen among the grasses and bushes.
name: Field beneath a Plateau

desc:
Some boulders, so big they must have been torn off of the Misty Mountains in the
far west and thrown here by giants, are spread on the ground. Dark mosses and
lichens cover the southeastern sides of all the boulders. The thick vegetation
does not seem to live; however it is not yet dead.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
The areas with dark grasses slouch moodily, it is obvious that something else is
weighing it down. A heavily sloping plain to the west prevents the eye from
being able to divulge what is on it in that direction as opposed to the fairly
unobstructed northern and eastern plains.
name: Plain with Slouching Grass

desc:
The valley makes a long, gentle bend, curving from east to north, and the trail
follows the curve of the valley, staying on the valley's floor. Rain and wind
have eroded the ground, washing soil away from between small rocks on the stony
ground.
name: Bend in an Eroded Trail

desc:
From here the trail leads shortly east before it seems to turn north again,
continuing in its major north-south direction. The valley becomes unusually wide
here with a field lying just beside the road. The brush-covered end of the
valley is visible further to the west. Some thick, impassable brush grows to the
north. Some gravel is lying on the ancient, mostly-unused trail.
name: Bend in a Trail through a Valley

desc:
The trail keeps zig-zagging southwest, following the valley between its steep,
unclimbable sides. From this deep down, however, little can be known about the
fields beyond the sides of the valley. Some eerie shadows from the large forest
to the southeast pass by now and then, as if the forest were alive.
name: Winding Trail

desc:
At this place the sides of the valley are particularly close to each other
creating a close passage that would unavoidably turn into a bottleneck if many
were to pass it at the same time. The narrowness of the area concentrates the
wind, clearing the area of high-growing vegetation. To the south, the trail
straightens, while its northern continuation winds to the northeast.
name: Narrow Passage on the Trail

desc:
Following the valley, the trail winds between the two plateaus. Beneath the
western side, a pile of debris tells the story of people unsuccessfully trying
to climb the wall or perhaps the result of some erosion. To the east, the grassy
plains ascend in a less steep manner.
name: Trail through a Valley

desc:
The trail curves here along the plateu to the west. The trail itself grows some
turf of grass here and there, but likely the trail is not totally without use.
The forest of Mirkwood looms farther to the east, and the air seems to be denser
and darker there than here.
name: Bend in a Trail

desc:
Just next to the escarpment to the west, a trail runs and bends from the east to
the south. A boulder, formerly embedded in the side of the escarpment, has
fallen down on the trail leaving a big hole where it formerly sat. However, the
hole is too high and still makes it impossible to climb up the steep wall.
name: Bend by a Boulder

desc:
This mudddy field is somewhat mushy underfoot but neither humanoids, nor animals
risk sinking down. Movement is not more difficult than in any other field,
except there is some risk for boots to become dirty. A large amount of
vegetative cover in the form of grasses and bushes grows here due to a hidden
water source. Some large, plodding animal's tracks can be seen in the mud.
name: Muddy Field

desc:
The grass of the field seems to have been burnt by the sun causing it to grow
very short, in a faded yellowish colour. To the west, the grass does not reach
over the ground, as it has been trampled by feet and hooves of some animal. To
the southeast the vegetation is healthier and several bushes can be seen.
name: Field of Yellowish Grass

desc:
The trees thin out to the east and end before reaching here. Some black
undergrowth thrives here, making the terrain very bushy, that at times can be
tiresome to pass through. To the north, the ground cover is replaced by mostly
dark grasses.
name: Brush-covered Field

desc:
Standing next to the plains, the trees that constitute this light forest do not
appear that gloomy. Further inside, however, there seems to be an impenetrable
darkness. To get to it though, another way must be found as there is a steep
slope blocking the path to the east, and an even steeper cliff wall to the
south.
name: Edge of Mirkwood

desc:
While there are some blackened tree trunks and bushes strewn about the area,
this field is mostly devoid of large vegation as it is so close to the forest of
Mirkwood. Some dark lichen can be seen creeping out of the forest, covering the
ground among the dark, gnarled roots. The grass disappears as a steep slope
leading to the west reveals another field.
name: Field next to a Forest

desc:
An enormous, hollow tree has fallen from the forest above, creating a strange
hovel-like structure where its bottom has been cracked open. The bones of
several animals can be seen strewn about in various states of decay. Very little
grass grows here in the sparse light due to the shadows cast by the forest on
the cliff above. The walls of the eastern cliffs are steep.
name: Decaying Tree in a Field

desc:
This enormous tree is gnarled, hideous, and reeks of decaying flesh and wood.
Black bark is coming off of it in sloughs and the trunk is completely covered
with yellow and orange fungi. The huge hollow of this rotting tree seems to have
been fashioned into a den by some giant, predatory creature. Large rotting bones
of various animals and humanoids are everywhere.
name: In a Rotten, Decaying Tree

desc:
The bushes to the south are so dense that nothing can be seen through them. An
ancient trail can be seen leading along the northern and western sides of the
plains in the distance. A dark forest spans across the eastern horizon, looking
awfully sinister.
name: Bushy Plains

desc:
The old, gravelly trail passes to the south and north here. On the trail there
are signs of a recent skirmish between unknown sides. Some blood markings on the
grass and marks on the ground are left as evidence. To the east lies a field
that sits opposite a trail running to the north and south.
name: Widening Trail

desc:
An old construction has resided next to the trail, maybe an old resting place or
trading post for travellers. Now the construction is only a pile of rubble
consisting of dark grey stones. There is an old campfire behind the rubble, long
since gone cold. The ancient, gravelly trail leads to the north, and turns here
towards the east and west, splitting when it meets a rising slope.
name: Crossing on a Trail

desc:
A trail leads through this brushland which offers very little nourishment to
attract many grasses. To the north, the brush seems to grow too high to allow
anyone to pass; but to the south brush turns into grasslands where the trail
continues in a latitudinal fashion.
name: Trail through Brushlands

desc:
Following the lower end of the slope to the east, the trail offers no passage to
the dark trees that cover the slope to the east. The trail leads on to the west
and south. Entering the forest of Mirkwood would require ascending up the large
cliff. However, the vertical cliff is not climbable.
name: Trail along a Slope

desc:
The trail continues along the rock face, atop which a few dark branches from the
Mirkwood Forest can be sighted reaching over the edge. The rock face doesn't
offer any footholds for someone wanting to try their luck ascending it.
Travellers who are going to the adjacent plain in the west will have an easy
trip.
name: Trail beneath Mirkwood

desc:
A small path twists its way nearby the forest. The path goes through a
depression in the plains. There is an open way through the depression in
north-south fashion. The depression is mostly of gravel and dirt, a few bigger
boulders lay on the side of the path too. To the east and west there are steep
bluffs, making a travel in those directions not viable.
name: Depression in a Path

desc:
A small path has formed at the border of the forest, forcing its way around
bushes and the many contorted roots that protrude from the undergrowth. A few
saplings grow here and there, proof of the continuous growth of the forest.
Black thorns grow from a few more mature sprouts.
name: Bend in a Path

desc:
These plains are unusually flat, as if they had been trampled by giants for
millennia, allowing a traveller to go across them without much troubley. To the
south, a fir tree can be seen standing solemly in a field, while a north-south
trail passes by to the east.
name: Flat Plains

desc:
A lone fir tree marks the landscape. Several vines have attached themselves to
the tree, ensnaring it slowly but surely. A few small trees have taken root here
as well, denoting the continuous forest growth. A large brush grows to the east,
its thorny branches spreading far.
name: At a Lone Fir Tree

desc:
The plains rise sharply to the north, ending in a cliff from which more plains
can be spotted, as well as two trails that lead through them. Dark grass
occupies most of the field, with a few plants growing here and there. A lone
tree lies to the east, the tallest that can be found for miles.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
Small bushes bearing few leaves form a small brush patch that grows larger to
the east. Many small ditches are to be found, some of them hidden by tall dark
green grass that grows together in many round patches. In some places no
vegetation can be found, showing only the earthen soil. Small pawprints are
found in the earth, although most are quite old.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
Next to the crossing to the east, there is a building, where whoever stayed,
could have guarded the passage or could seek cover from weather and attacking
foes. Now it is only a ruin without a roof with walls that are little more than
piles of stones. Past it to the south, the unkept road continues through a
valley pass.
name: Ruin on the Decaying Road

desc:
Several spiny bushes cover the bleak earth, most over three feet tall and over
six feet wide. They bear no berries, only sharp black thorns that bear crimson
dots. A small skeleton of a bird lies on top of one of the bushes, perforated by
a few of the thorns.
name: Thick Brushwood

desc:
Several thorny branches of nearby bushes form an intricate maze, preventing
travellers from smoothly pass through it. Gnarled roots, an extension of the
forest to the east, form a natural barrier to the south and east. The roots are
grey in colour. The ends that stick out of the undergrowth are sometimes sharp,
sometimes almost completely shrivelled.
name: Thick Brushwood

desc:
Close by the forest, a myriad of different shrubs and bushes have taken root,
taking the nutrients from the soil that were denied them in the forest itself.
Many brambles and little fruit is found on the shrubs, their thorns coloured a
deep shade of red, resembling blood. Many of the thorns litter the undergrowth,
urging travellers with light feet protection to proceed with caution.
name: Thick Brushwood

desc:
An overgrown path curves next to a forest border, leading travellers south and
west. The dark forest seems to be alive, seen most clearly by small, pitch-black
roots twisting on the path. Encircling the path are small bubbling puddles of
grey mud. A few roots that have ventured in the mud have shrivelled even more
than usual, and lie there forever still.
name: Path through Dense Undergrowth

desc:
The path twists and turns through the encroaching undergrowth that seems
destined to swallow it. A few bones, possibly humanoid, lie in a small pile,
half-hidden under the roots of an upturned tree. The tree bark is heavily
damaged, marked by several axes and swords. An axe blade has been left behind in
the tree, covered in rust.
name: Path by a Barricade

desc:
Little more than gravel is left of the road that once ran through the plains.
Half a mile to the south of the old road a cliff dominates the view. To the east
an extremely steep slope leads upwards to a plateau in the plains.
name: Decaying Road

desc:
Twisting and turning, the old road makes its last visible sharp bend on its way
through the plains. From here on, the road seems to run straight into the
distance to the south. To the north and west the plains rise too sharply to
allow climbing, but the road to the east continues on level ground.
name: Unkept Road

desc:
Grey stones have been put together in ages past to create this large road. The
road is in an abominable state, heavily overgrown, and it bears many chipped and
broken stones. The earth declines to the south, while it slowly increases to the
west. A large bush stands next to the road, leafless and without fruit.
name: Unkept Road

desc:
Many small tufts of grass form a great patch, increasing in size to the east.
The earth slants upwards as well, ending in a small cliff to the north. The
south and the east are blocked by steep slopes, which leaves the west, where
small white stones form a road.
name: Cut-off Field

desc:
Withered brown and yellow grass can be found everywhere, with small patches of
lush grass growing in between them. A small patch of weeds with purple flowers
grows near a small knoll. Many small pebbles litter the limestone soil, leading
to the east where they meet a road. The west becomes quite steep, the ridges too
far apart to be crossed without climbing skills.
name: Withered Field

desc:
A large road, unkempt and little-travelled, leads travellers south and upwards
towards the north. Weeds growing under the bricks have broken them in two or
more pieces. Due to its state of decay, the road is now coloured partly green or
dark grey, with limited amounts of the original white still showing slightly.
Beside the weeds, some tall grasses grow here, adding more green colour to the
landscape.
name: Unkept Road

desc:
A medium-sized road, half-hidden by vegetation that has had decades to fester.
The large stones that form the road are mostly intact, as the way is not well
travelled. Patches of low grass grow beside the road, intertwined with few
clover, daffodils, and buttercups, each adding a coloured twist to the otherwise
green field.
name: Overgrown Road

desc:
Passing a drinking pond to the west, the road continues to the north and the
south, although it slopes heavily there. Many different rocks litter the ground,
some of them covered with green moss. A few birch and apple trees grow here,
providing shelter and shade from the forces of nature.
name: Overgrown Road

desc:
The field slopes heavily upwards to the west, although the way there is not very
steep. This is not the case to the north, where it is impossible to advance
further due to the strong elevation. The soil here is covered with green and
brown grasses, with small trees steadily growing upwards. A road lies to the
east, although it is heavily overgrown.
name: Sloping Field

desc:
The earth gently slopes upwards to the north, whilst a more rugged slope leads
downwards to the east. The field is quite treacherous, having hundreds of small
pits, most of which are partially overgrown by the local flora and thick moss.
name: Treacherous Field

desc:
Grass grows all around, in different lengths and colours. On the western side,
the blades are a verdant green with an exceptionally sharp edge, growing quite
closely together in a large patch of grass. On the opposite side, the grass is
at least three inches wide with a much paler complexion. One small white stone
lies abandoned to the east.
name: Mixed Grassy Field

desc:
The ground is quite uneven, creating a landscape of many small hills. A pond has
formed here, where the local fauna comes to drink, as can be seen by the many
animal tracks near the edge of the water and bite marks on twigs and leaves.
Many thick bushes thrive here, along with a myriad of different plants and
flowers.
name: Drinking Pond

desc:
Thousands of small cracks with holes over one inch wide can be found in the
black, earthen soil. Where there are cracks, there is little or no vegetation,
and what little vegetation is here, is mostly stunted or charred. A few small
pools have formed here, although most of them are little more than thick mud.
Thick bubbles pop up on the pool surface, emitting a foul odour.
name: Cracked Earth

desc:
A lone arrow sticks out in the centre of the field, firmly planted into the
soil. No vegetation surrounds it, making it an excellent marker. A few lone
beech trees add some variation to the otherwise plain landscape, mostly filled
with long grasses and thick brush. The earth is mostly flat, yet laden with many
small cracks.
name: Sparse Field

desc:
Many small white stones have been put together to form a road that runs through
these plains. Small roots, belonging to a large oak tree, stick out of a few
stones. Many flowers grow on both sides of the road, a rainbow of colours. The
earth slopes upwards to the north.
name: Overgrown Road

desc:
Large, white stones close together formed a road once in the past. What remains
today are grey and green coloured stones that lie scattered around, broken by
nature and time. The road itself is still partly intact, yet unable to suffer
large and heavy weights, such as caravans or merchant carts.
name: Half-broken Road

desc:
Large square stones that have turned grey and dark green with age and wear form
a road leading down to the south and northwards. Many small weeds grow in and
around it, showing that the road is not well-maintained. Large fields can be
found on either side of the road, sloping heavily.
name: Broken Road

desc:
Many small mounds form quite uneven ground on which this road has been built.
The plants and grass that grow on these mounds are somehow more lush than those
growing elsewhere. A few thornbushes grow west of the road in a strange pattern,
one that resembles a skull. The road continues to the north and the south,
whilst a cliff can be seen to the east.
name: Broken Road

desc:
The road that runs west and north here lies in total decay. Many stones have
upturned and several of them are broken in half due to growing weeds below. The
weeds and dark green moss cover the stones, marking the road as one not well
maintained or travelled.
name: Broken Road

desc:
Small grey bricks, chipped and broken, together form a little-maintained road.
Grey moss and weeds grow between the stones, almost covering them completely. On
the western edge of the road, an abandoned horseshoe catches the eye. The road
ascends sharply to the west, leading travellers upwards, whilst to the east the
uneven ground mostly remains on the same level.
name: Broken Road

desc:
Leading north and south, a heavily worn road can be found here. Most of the once
white and now grey stones are covered almost entirely with grass, moss, and
weeds. A few mushrooms grow beside it as well, differing in colour. The ground
slopes heavily downwards to the north.
name: Worn Road

desc:
A grassy field lies south, whilst in other parts the land is quite barren, save
for a few bushes and weeds. The ground slants up and down irregularly, creating
an unsteady footing. The grass reaches no more than a foot in height, growing in
patches a few feet wide. A few small animal-dug holes are found in the earth,
hinting of wildlife close by. A road can be seen to the west.
name: Slanting Field

desc:
Many small ridges that are quite close together forming small steps that lead
travellers randomly up and down. The ridges also create two stairways, leading
to the valley floor below and upwards to a cliff. A few small trees grow in
between the grass, although they bear no leaves. The field continues to the
west.
name: Rolling Field

desc:
The surface of the cliff is minimal, only allowing room for a few creatures to
stand upon it. Crab grass grows all over, yet it is the only vegetation to be
found. Looking below, small ridges form a steep path below, although it would be
an arduous climb. On the western edge, a sharp rock stands, shaped like an S.
name: Top of a Cliff

desc:
The earth seems to have almost split in two, creating two small hills with a
twisting line in the middle. Apart from a lone tree and a few thick bushes,
grass is the dominant growth. It varies greatly in height and colour, from being
grazed and lush green not so long ago to only a few dark brown inches.
name: Uneven Ground

desc:
Aside from a lone birch tree, this field is laden with weeds, varying greatly in
size and form, some little more than woody thorns, while others near the size of
young bushes. The leafage is mottled with uneven black spots with hints of brown
and red in them. A few small twigs lie scattered on the ground, bleak and
leafless.
name: Weed-infested Field

desc:
Short, brown grass grows on a small slope in this barren steppe, the only large
patch of vegetation to be found, besides lone patches of weeds. Small rocks
litter the cracked earth, which rises up in sharp contrast, creating a heavily
uneven ground. A few animal leavings lie to the north, where the soil is much
more thick and lush.
name: Uneven Ground

desc:
A carcass, devoid of meat, lies half-hidden amongst tall, mottled grass. Over
two feet high, the grass is uneaten by the local fauna, perhaps due to the odd
black specks that mark the grass. It does rustle continuously, hinting of lower
lifeforms that populate the ground. In a few places, the grass has been
trampled, hinting of higher lifeforms as well.
name: Field of Mottled Grass

desc:
In the centre of this area are a couple of blackberry bushes, surrounded by
medium-sized grass. Across the field, a few patches have been grazed clean,
leaving nothing for the eye but a bare patch of earth. A few animal droppings
litter the ground, although they appear to be quite old.
name: Lush Field

desc:
The grass is exceptionally verdant here, something that has not escaped the
local fauna, as most of it has been trimmed by bites, leaving only few slightly
more brown patches that have grown tall. The green parts only reach a few inches
high, where infant bushes and plants have leave to steal their sunlight and
forever leave them stunted.
name: Grazed Field

desc:
Several lush grasses grow here, some having grown over three feet tall, as yet
untouched by the wildlife. The grass is mostly green in colour, although near
the western end, some have become mottled and brown. A small, muddy black puddle
has formed there, the grass that stands in it black and withered.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
Jagged rocks litter this grassy field, scattered like disarrayed pawns on a
chessboard. A few rocks bear lichen and moss, while a particular rock in the
middle has had its edges honed. Thick animal hairs can be found scattered on the
ground beside it, proving that it is often visited by the local wildlife. A few
boulders stand close together to the south, blocking a direct path southwards.
name: Rocky Field

desc:
A few pine trees loom here, surrounded by thick brushwood. Many of the pine
needles litter the ground, their sharp ends adding to the thorns present on the
branches of the bushes. Further away from the trees, the bushes grow more apart,
allowing one to spy more details. Their thorns are razor-sharp and oddly, barbed
as well.
name: Thorny Brushwood

desc:
Small hills of sandy ground populate this area, each small hill bearing one or
two small bushes. Grass over one foot high grows in a small patch near the
southern edge, leaving the rest bare, in total contrast to the surroundings.
While the south becomes more grassy, the vegetation thickens everywhere else,
only allowing passage northwards.
name: Sandy Field

desc:
Medium-sized bushes grow abundantly, centring around a small puddle of clear
water. A few roots stick out of the earth and dive directly into the middle of
it. Succulent, verdant blades of grass grow between the bushes, some of the
tufts already trampled or devoured by the passing wildlife. The vegetation
increases and becomes more dense in almost every direction, forcing travellers
to head westwards to escape this area.
name: Wet Brushland

desc:
Mottled brown and green grass from the west meets thick brushwood here, a mix of
high and thorny bushes. In the centre is a muddy hole of over four feet in
diameter, with multiple animal footprints leading out of it. Many bubbles can be
seen in the mud, one having popped at this exact second.
name: Muddy Hole

desc:
Three-foot high, thick bushes prevail over the lower-lying vegetation, which has
largely diminished to due the stolen sunlight and soil nourishment. The bushes
concentrate heavily to the south and the west, blocking any direct passageway.
One small bush lies alone to the east, blacker and more withered than its
comrades.
name: High Bushes

desc:
The thick brushwood is cut in half as the earth slopes up and down continuously.
A couple of the bushes that are directly on top of the slope, have their roots
protrude from out of the earth. Due to the uneven ground, the area is quite
difficult to traverse. The brush continues on for miles in every direction.
name: Bushy, Uneven Ground

desc:
Thick, thorny stems grow all around, colouring the area green and brown. A few
bushes are different than the others. Instead of having grown tall and lush,
they are crooked, with withered leaves that emit a foul odour. They are centred
around the northwest, yet slowly spread to the rest of the brush.
name: Thorny Bushes

desc:
This small area is infested with thick brush and thorny plants, impeding swift
movement. The vegetation is the thickest westward, where it is impossible to
move through. A few young trees grow northwards, where they meet the forest. To
the east, the vegetation becomes paler and less thick, bushes clearing the way
for grass.
name: Thick Brush

desc:
Great, crooked birch and pine trees cover the entire area, their branches
flaying in every direction regardless of the wind as they block the sunlight
coming from above. The foliage below is pale and stunted, struggling to survive.
Thick, blackish moss grows on the trees, along with a few fungi.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
Tall stands of pale yellow grass grows in dense clusters before slowly fading
towards a thick, gloomy forest towards the north and east. Below the grass,
smaller plants struggle in vain for sunlight, and have become pale in colour and
spindly in height in their attempts to grow upwards. A few bushes have managed
to struggle through the high grass and flourish in the moist soil.
name: Tall Grasses

desc:
The forest begins to get very dark as an air of despair sets in. The trees have
a blackened appearance among their trunks and the vegetation of the forest floor
has a dark hue to it. It seems as though no sunlight is allowed to travel
through the forest ceiling and the ground foliage thrives on malevolence rather
than sunlight.
name: Edge of a Dismal Forest

desc:
Fungi of all sorts populate this part of the forest. There are mushrooms of all
colours and huge shelf fungi grow on the trunks of the dark oak and beech trees.
The fungus grows so thick here that it has created a flora all its own on the
forest floor with chunks of fungal crust covering the ground. The spores are so
thick here that they cloud the very air like a noxious fog.
name: Fungus Patch

desc:
Gloom fills the air in these woods. There are looming trees in every direction,
which have borne the weight of this despair for many years. The trunks here are
a dark shade of grey, as is the ground surrounding them. There are grey
mushrooms on the ground all about.
name: Gloomy Woods

desc:
The trees, which loom in every direction, open up into a small glade here. There
is an air of serenity and calm here, as if the malice and gloom that permeate
Mirkwood are held at bay. Some mushrooms grow in the area, clustering more
densely toward the south.
name: Opening in a Gloomy Forest

desc:
The gloomy forest makes a natural corridor as the trees loom in on every side.
Twisted patterns seem to form angry faces in the bark. There are thick, heavy
cobwebs hanging from the limbs just overhead. The way west and south is blocked
by the huge trunks of the twisted trees that grow there.
name: Forested Corridor

desc:
The overbearing forest thins just enough here to make it passable, but these
traveller's gains are not without penance. Jagged rocks stab forth from the
cloak of thorny brush and decaying matter that make up the forest floor, slicing
toward the veiled sky. The land slopes slightly downward to the south, where the
trees look sickly and pale.
name: Thinning Forest

desc:
The pale, sickly trees here are coated with a thin, dark slime, from which
issues the putrid smell of decay and corruption. Their branches reach
plaintively toward a sky that is unremittingly just out of reach. The ground
underfoot has turned to thick, black mud that sucks at the feet of anything
attempting to traverse it, making movement difficult. Grasping, choking vines
encrusted with razor-sharp barbed thorns adorn every branch still harbouring
enough life to support them.
name: Putrid, Slimy Forest

desc:
A thick matting of fallen leaves covers the ground under the stooping canopy of
trees above. A watch post has been created here under the dense foliage. Soft
piles of grass lie strategically placed to allow comfort during a long pass of
keeping watch. The foliage to the east, north, and west might appear
impenetrable at first glance but cleverly arranged branches and leaves allow a
view of the trail beyond, albeit somewhat restricted.
name: Hidden Watch-post

desc:
Sickly pine trees grow here, their boughs hanging low as if in defeated dismay.
The tall, dismal pines creak and groan as their rotting trunks shift unendingly.
Above, their ragged canopies have grown together, forming a thick cover that
blot out the sky.
name: Sagging Pine Trees

desc:
Tall pine trees with their boughs sagging from some unseen weight grow here in a
look of eternal sadness. The ground to the northeast begins to become soggy and
a putrid smell wafts in from that direction. The miserable pine trees continue
their mourning to the east and west.
name: Sagging Pines

desc:
Sorrowful pine trees grow here, populating the west part of this section of the
forest. Dense bushes make up the undergrowth to the southeast, making the forest
completely impassable. The northern area is soggy and wet, as a swamp lies in
that direction.
name: Sagging Pines

desc:
Thick, healthy beech trees grow here, many with significantly brighter shades of
colour on their bark. The beech trees grow so thick to the west because of the
abundant sunlight that is available there that they create a vegetation wall
that is impassable.
name: Thick Beech Forest

desc:
Large, expansive beech trees meet sad, sagging pines which invade with their
forlorn, stationary procession from the north. Sticky brambles and huge,
demented thorn bushes expand from the east causing a meeting of the different
types of vegetation in the middle of this beech tree forest.
name: Beech Forest

desc:
Large thorn bushes and tangled brambles grow in a flourish here. Their leaves
secrete a sticky black sap which sticks fast to everything it comes into contact
with. The thornbushes have huge thorns the size of small eagle talons which
threaten to jab at the slightest breath of air.
name: Sticky Brush

desc:
Towering trees, hundreds of feet tall, spread heavy branches that let pass no
hint of the sky above. These giants so dominate their surroundings that no
vegetation grows beneath them other than a rugged and tangled mass of roots,
each as thick as a man is tall. Between ground and canopy there is nothing but
an expanse of heavy, oppressive darkness.
name: Under a Dark Canopy

desc:
The ground here is very bumpy and difficult to grasp a foothold on. There are
roots of every size and thickness growing all about as if there is not enough
soil to share between them. There are many roots which normally would be growing
underground, but are exposed to the forest air.
name: Exposed Roots

desc:
There is a low point of elevation in the forest here that collects the mist that
seems to float all about this area. There are wisps of some transparent forms
which float to and fro. Among the trees that grow surrounding this depression
can be heard some strange whispering sounds of unseen beings.
name: Haunted Depression

desc:
Dead trees still rooted in the ground dominate this section of the forest,
giving it an eerie appearance. Rotting branches on the ground encourages insect
infestations and give off a rotten smell. Barely enough branches with blackened
leaves remain on the dead trees to shield the ground from direct sunlight.
name: Dead Trees

desc:
A slight rise from the west gives way to leaning, twisted oak trees among which
the whispers of shifting branches can faintly be heard. The ground is covered in
a thick layer of blackened leaves. Faint whispers of fog swirl around the oak
tree's boles, only to dissipate as soon as they begin to take shape.
name: Haunted Oak Trees

desc:
Huge trunks with blackened bark, these ancient oak trees grow with strange,
malevolent vibrancy. The tops of the trees are quite high here, blotting out any
sunlight that may attempt to reach the rotting flora below. The air is
oppressively humid from the moisture which slowly collects from the swamps to
the west.
name: Great Black Oak Trees

desc:
The forest floor is quite lumpy here as the swamp to the north dissipates itself
into this part of the forest. There are dry patches on which great black oak
trees grow as small tributaries from the swamp lie in natural moats around them,
with stagnant, stinking water.
name: Swampy Forest

desc:
The thick trunks of darkened trees stand in silent watch over the soft,
leaf-covered ground. A faint sound fills the air, as if hundreds of voices were
constantly whispering from very far away, but no words or meaning can be
discerned.
name: Haunted Forest

desc:
Dark and gloomy oak trees hang overhead within a shadowed canopy. Not much can
be seen here besides the huge tree trunks which obstruct the view in any
direction beyond a few strides. Large fungi grow here out of the duff layer,
which is mainly composed of tree roots and fallen branches. A constant moaning
sound can be heard off to the west.
name: Dark Oak Trees

desc:
Ancient, twisted oak trees grow here with a tortured appearance. Huge, malignant
looking burls grow on the sides of the trunks, looking like some tortured
goblin-faces. There is a terrible stink in the air as if there is disease
lurking about here.
name: Twisted Oak Trees

desc:
A soft moaning can faintly be heard, seeming to originate from within the dark
oaks that stand here. A faint smell fills the air, a smell of rot and decay and
wet vegetation. The leaves on many of the oaks are unnaturally black, making
them even more effective at creating an impenetrable canopy.
name: Haunted Oaks

desc:
Decrepit vegetation and decaying flowers indicate the remnants of an ancient
garden. If a garden was ever tended in this place, it has now become a breeding
ground for atrocious plants that thrive in the oppressive evil of this part of
the forest. The ground gets soft to the west.
name: Wicked Garden

desc:
The trees rooted here in the muck are characterized more by decay than life.
Their black, limp leaves hang from drooping branches and the smell of death and
rotting vegetation hangs in the air. There are quite possibly the remains of
dead animals or humanoids sunken under the mud and dead plants.
name: Decaying, Muddy Forest

desc:
Still swamp water sits here with floating, rotting vegetation on its surface. A
few trees grow feebly in the waist deep water, dropping their dark-coloured
leaves to add to the rotting muck floating about them. To the west the mire is
not as deep and is replaced by a surface of stinking muck.
name: Stagnant Swamp in a Forest

desc:
The mud here is so thick that it is exhausting to travel through. A nasty smell
hangs in the air as of death and overgrowth of a swamp. The filth here seems to
suck in and devour all that it comes into contact with, making it very difficult
to navigate a safe path through the treacherous mire.
name: Sinking Muck

desc:
The forest gives way to dark, muddy water that continues south and west. No
light penetrates the thick, chaotic web of thorn-encrusted vines that bind the
forest canopy together. Wretched, pale trees reach plaintively toward an
invisible sky. The water appears to be filled with shoals of eyeless fish, spiny
forms that dart back and forth just under the surface.
name: Stagnant Swamp in a Forest

desc:
Trees here reach for the sky in a futile attempt to escape the poisonous swamp
water, yet some of the plant life here seems to thrive on the life-stealing
properties of the swamp. The forlorn trees look to give way to a mixed muck and
few or no trees to the south. The muck to the south could be dangerous as it
appears that it could suck an unwary traveller under.
name: Stagnant Swamp in a Forest

desc:
Enormous gnarled roots grow all about here as if to protect the cavern which
they have carved out below. The roots have grown in a pattern in which to cause
a natural wall in all directions except for the curtain-like growth of
vegetation made of dead roots to the east. The smell of death lingers in the
thick air as faint sounds of movement can be heard from below.
name: Foul Cavern

desc:
Roots are growing all over in every direction here but there is space enough to
fit through. The roots are strong and can withstand a large amount of weight.
The experienced climber could use the roots growing all about in this vertical
tunnel almost as a ladder.
name: Ladder of Roots

desc:
Padded and moist clumps of moss group thick in this cavern, some giving off an
iridescent glow. A hollow wind can be heard coming from above, from a vertical
tunnel. There are a few roots hanging down from the tunnel, providing a means
for climbing up. The moss seems to move about slowly in some of the darker,
tighter corners of the cavern. The cave opens up to a round tunnel to the east.
name: Mossy Cavern

desc:
The thick moss continues to grow all about in this round tunnel. Burly chunks of
moss, which could no longer be held fast to the tunnel roof, have begun to break
free and hang down. The hanging moss sways back and forth as the wind blows
through the tunnel.
name: Hollow Tunnel

desc:
The moss grows somewhat thinner here but is still quite prevalent. The cave has
an unusually symmetrical shape to it. It is obvious that a malevolent force has
carved this cavern out of the earth. There is a thick covering of dark green
moss on sections of the cavern floor.
name: Back of a Cave

desc:
A soggy bed of moss lines the bottom of this dark, cavernous pit. The moss seems
to thrive in the moisture which sits stagnant in here. There are places here and
there within the cavern where the water is deep and then shallow, though the
mossy bed obscures much of the floor. In several places in the eastern part of
the cavern's ceiling, there are long, narrow cracks where the sunlight that
allows the moss to thrive comes in.
name: Water-soaked Bed of Moss

desc:
Spikes of varying sizes almost completely cover the earthen floor. What space
lies between them is occupied by bones of different creatures, although the
majority of them are humanoid. Most of the spikes found here are very sharp,
although over time, the edges have become more dull. This has been countered by
rust however, and the wooden spikes that have broken still bear somewhat of a
rough side that would invoke pain on those that land upon them.
name: Spike Trap

desc:
The plains resemble ripples, sloping up and down irregularly, gradually
increasing in level to the east until it ends in a cliff. From the top, thick
brushwood can be spotted on the other side, blocking the view. The cliff itself
is quite laden with lush grass, with small black flowers in between. A small
hole has been dug in the earth, pawprints clearly visible in the small mound of
dirt beside it.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
Many small twigs, dried brown leaves, and a large number of long, brown hairs
form a mediocre soft footing in this earthen cavern. Gnawed bones, not entirely
devoid of meat, lie scattered on the leaves, to be devoured when hunger strikes
the creatures inhabiting the cave. The meat has started to rot however, emitting
a foul smell. The cave slopes upwards to the west, marking the exit.
name: Wolf Lair

desc:
A few maple trees grow in a small grove on a ridge to the east, some of their
roots protruding from the earth due to the erosion. Some of the bark has been
cut from the trees, providing a sharp contrast between the dark and light brown
colours. Lemongrass is abundant here, growing between the normal grass. Some of
the plants have been uprooted, and lie discarded on the northern side of the
plain.
name: Eroded Plain

desc:
A large boulder rests in the centre of this area, surrounded by sickly, thin
grass, as sunlight nearly never falls directly on it. The surrounding vegetation
is a bit more lush, and more varied also. A few small and medium-sized bushes
are scattered about, and an oddly formed tree, resembling a willow, provides
shadow in the south.
name: Sickly Field

desc:
A cave entrance lies directly to the east, its walls an eerie dark. Dark
coloured grass, more black than green, grows in front of it. Old pawprints can
be seen on some trampled grass blades, at least a few inches in diameter. A
large birch tree adds shadow to the field, although its bark has been heavily
damaged by what could be claws. A road can be seen far to the west.
name: Outside a Cave

desc:
The earth suddenly slopes up and down, creating a rippling effect. A field of
tall, green grass like any other can be found here, except for one detail. A
large carcass devoid of sinew, meat, and fur stands in a barren spot. No grass
grows around it and the earth by it is dry and cracked, which makes it very
noticeable.
name: Sloping Field

desc:
A small valley is created as the earth slopes down towards the middle of the
field. A few medium-sized trees grow here, although they are quite far apart
from each other. Some trees have taken root on a rather steep section, their
roots protruding from the earth on one side. The southern part of this area
bears little to no resemblance to the rest of the field. The grass that is
supposed to thrive is dried-out and a matted brown, the earth a slippery black.
name: Small Valley

desc:
Sloping sharply up and down, mounds resembling small hills are found everywhere.
To the east, the ground becomes steeper with every step, slowly leading
downwards. A miniscule stream drips from a small ridge into a small puddle.
Thick bushes and dark green weeds thrive here, those below nourished by the wet
ground and those above by the light of the sun.
name: Sloping Field

desc:
Many small ridges create a heavily uneven ground. It declines sharply to the
east, forcing one to climb down to proceed. A few large boulders lie to the
south, each having a large bush between them. Many different grasses and plants
occupy this field, allowing one to spend hours identifying them all.
name: Falling Ridge

desc:
A stump of a giant tree marks the centre of this area, heavily overgrown by
weeds and surrounded by thick bushes. Chipped bark and small twigs cover the
tracks of the tree, which appears to have been felled and dragged away
afterwards. One thick, rotten branch lies in the northern part, covered by a
swarm of insects.
name: Giant Tree Stump

desc:
A mix of refuse, rotten food, and large and small bones lies all together with
various other garbage on a majestic pile. A ghastly odour emanates from the
pile, which would affect even the strongest. The garbage pile covers the entire
field, almost tipping over the edge of the palisade to the north. A humanoid
skull lies at the bottom of the pile, a crack in the middle of the forehead.
name: Giant Pile of Garbage

desc:
This small hut bears more comfort than the rest of the camp, as it is furnished
with a large rug on the floor, a few chairs, and a large desk. A few meatless
bones and an empty pot of stew are on a tray on the desk. A great tapestry
adorns the eastern wall, depicting a battlefield. A latched door is visible to
the north.
name: Commander's Quarters

desc:
Small cracks litter the boards that form this makeshift watchtower. The view
outside is partly blocked by a thick bush, although also hiding the tower from
view. A few vines penetrate the wooden structure, coiling on the floor. Through
a small trapdoor in the northern corner, a ladder protrudes through, the only
logical exit down.
name: Rickety Watchtower

desc:
A small path leads from the western wall to a wooden door to the south. There is
very little room to manoeuvre, as the huts that lie to the west were built quite
closely against the eastern palisade. A few small bones lie below, a layer of
dust and earth upon them. An abandoned pot, smelling faintly of stew, lies
against the wooden wall of the western hut.
name: Hidden Path

desc:
A few oak trees scattered around the field, present the only protection against
the weather elements. A small number of them are diseased however, their leaves
and branches withering slowly. The soil around the trees is cracked and dry,
which has only allowed the most persistent weeds to grow.
name: Small Stand of Oak Trees

desc:
Long tufts of grass cover the soil, hiding the many sharp rocks that lie
scattered around. In a few places, the earth is dry, cracked and nearly devoid
of vegetation. Small plants with razor-sharp thorns grow out of many of the
cracks, yet most of them have already withered completely, having become nothing
more than brown husks.
name: Dry Field

desc:
Many small holes can be found in these plains, most half hidden by tall green
grasses. The holes are countered by mounds of dirt, a few thorny bushes growing
on them. A round patch of grass lies to the south, no longer green but a greyish
black. Beyond the grassy patch, a tree stump can be spotted.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
A few pine trees grow in the middle of this field, clouding the undergrowth in
an array of green needles. Thick scratch marks are clearly noticeable on some of
the trees, belonging to a creature with big claws. Eastwards, the earth slowly
slopes upwards in a twisting manner, making it too difficult to ascend. To the
west, the descent is rather brusque.
name: Field with Pine Trees

desc:
This large grassy field is quite elevated from the lands to the north and the
west, giving travellers a splendid view. The flora present greatly varies from
tall, rustling grasses to a myriad of different plants and flowers. A large
anthill lies near the earthen wall to the east, bustling with activity.
name: Grassy Field with a View

desc:
Many rocks, both large and small but equally sharp, lie to the west, the result
of a rockslide. Close by, a dried up pond can be seen here, only having
undrinkable dregs of muddy water remaining. At the bottom of the valley, the
only way out is upwards, moving along the rocks and ridges. It looks like
someone has been here, there are traces that the mud in the bottom has been
moved.
name: Bottom of a Valley

desc:
Upon this ledge, located almost at the top of the valley, it is possible to have
an amazing view of the valley below. Many small rocks stick out of the western
ledge, enclosed by the ridge as it continues upwards to the west in a smooth
unclimbable overhang. Small tufts of grass grow everywhere, some of them grazed
by animal, hinting of recent life visiting this ledge.
name: High on a Ledge

desc:
This cave is about forty feet high at the entrance to the east, while the
ceiling is lower to the north and west. A colony of bats cling to the cave's
roof, while on the floor thick piles of guano teem with cockroaches and worms.
The air is quite humid inside the cave, and there is a strong smell of guano and
other things.
name: Smelly Cave

desc:
A multitude of moon-pale stalagmites stand in this cave. In between the
stalagmites there are animal furs and piles of straw and leaves humped together
on the floor. There is enough space for very big creatures to stand here easily
and to lie down on the soft piles of furs and vegetation.
name: Smelly Side Chamber

desc:
The ground is highly uneven, resembling many small paths that twist and turn
with every footstep. Small tufts of grass and low shrubs of heather populate the
field, although thick bushes can be seen growing in a few places. Upon a small
mound, a cluster of blue flowers thrives. The earth in the centre of the field
bears a few cracks, most over a feet wide. A thick, twisted root protrudes from
one of the cracks, although there is no tree to whom it could belong to in
sight.
name: Rolling Field

desc:
The area is heavily divided. The southern part is covered with black earth, from
which many small saplings grow. The total opposite can be found to the north,
where the vegetation is quite thick, with a bed of flowers and weeds,
surrounding a small patch of bushes and fir trees. A small mound devoid of
vegetation bears a small hole, the home of a small animal.
name: Bushy Field

desc:
A once robust tree grove has turned into a bleak field, due to a fire long past.
No leaves remain on the trees, which vary in colours; some are white as ash,
others charred black, and many are in between. The scorched earth is mostly
barren, although a few tufts of grass remain, they bear no green lushness. The
earth slopes steeply upwards to the south and the east, barring the way.
name: Bleak Field

desc:
Patches of scorched grass are followed by layers of grey and white ash,
rendering the area quite desolate. A few bushes that grew lush in the past, now
have become grey-coloured, leafless branches. A giant fir tree that stood in the
centre of the field upon a small hill, has burnt down almost completely, leaving
only half a trunk, its sharp edges pointing upwards.
name: Burnt Field

desc:
A few scorched birch trees stand their ground, surrounded by solid black,
charred grass. The entire field is coloured bleak; black, grey, and earth-brown
predominate. Thick weeds, resistant to fire, grow closely together on a small
slope to the north. On the western side, upon a small rise, a few green buds
have begun to sprout amidst the burnt ground, indicating that the fire that
swept through this area is not all that recent.
name: Burnt Field

desc:
The rise in the grassy plains is still rather moderate compared to the flat
field to the east, but many yards below the trail that passes by the west and
east. The heather that grows here is very sparse and rough. A single tree grows
here, and there is a bird's nest on a branch.
name: Bluff Overlooking Two Trails

desc:
Compared to the open plains to the west, that stretch all the way to the great
Anduin river in the far distance, the surroundings quickly get gloomier in the
forest. Because of the compact darkness, it is impossible to tell how far the
forest continues to the southeast. A dense patch of briars can be seen growing
in the north that forms a nearly impenetrable wall.
name: Glooming Forest

desc:
Dark undergrowth of a brush-like kind covers the ground, actually making the
underlying soil impossible to see. Towards the south, the undergrowth gradually
reaches taller, to the point where it is no longer best described as
undergrowth. The dark forest seems impassable to other direction except to the
south.
name: Brush with Dark Undergrowth

desc:
The trees have become dense and dark enough that the surroundings feel eerily
dangerous. This marks the beginning of the great forest that reaches further to
the east than the eye can see. The forest to the northwest is sparser and it
might be a good direction to head out of here. The ground still holds some signs
of a field but gradually becomes more covered with roots.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Massive roots rupture through the ground, offering many opportunities for a
traveller to fall and break their legs. They twist their way around each other
entangling and turning into the undergrowth-covered earth. Black trees grow
about the area, heavily covered with mosses, lichen, fungi, and fallen leaves.
name: Root-covered Forest Floor

desc:
The vegetation in the area is extremely thick and dark. There is no obvious way
out of the dark forest clearing stretching out to the southeast. The forest here
is much deeper than that of its surroundings, almost as if it were a pit, and is
almost enclosed in a wall-like structure of sheer boulders, gnarled roots, and
prickly bushes. The ground is moist and gives off a rotting smell from the
putrid undergrowth that is covered with various species of fungus.
name: Dark Depression in the Forest

desc:
Cuts from different kinds of weapons on some of the gnarled tree trunks tell the
story of a skirmish that took place under the canopy here. Some splintered
arrows can be found on the ground in the dim lighting. Travelling west is made
impossible by a row of massive boulders, but with some time it should be
possible to get out through some prickly thorn bushes to the south.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The murky, vegetation takes many shapes as it rises upwards from the moss-
covered, wet forest ground. The undergrowth rises a yard above the moss,
consisting nearly entirely of black ferns whose stems containing only the
slightest hint of visible green chlorophyll. Above everything else, the enormous
trees of Mirkwood Forest rise with their twisted trunks reaching upwards and
never letting any sunshine through.
name: Thick Vegetation in a Forest

desc:
Several decaying animal corpses are here providing nutrition to the blackened
lichen and dark mushrooms that cover the forest floor. It doesn't seem possible
to travel through the trees to the southeast but going north seems feasible.
Grasses grow among the gnarled roots that cover the forest floor.
name: Mushroom-covered Forest

desc:
The trees here seem to feed off of the darkness because they grow impossibly
higher with very little light. The vegetation is so dense that even someone
accustomed to seeing in the dark would have difficulties judging the distance
between trees. Filling in the space between the tree trunks to the north are
some high bushes. The northern dark brush seems possible to push aside, but
coming back may be more difficult.
name: Pitch Black Forest

desc:
This part of the forest holds trees higher than the surrounding areas, as if
they refused to bow down to the great darkness being exuded far away to the
southeast. Combined with the denseness of the trees to the north, their height
gives the impression of a wall reaching all the way up to the sky.
name: Tall Forest

desc:
Mirkwood Forest grows very dense here, creating darkness as its canopy blocks
all light, even though it is the edge of the forest. Just a few yards from the
western cliff wall, where the forest ends, the trees get so numerous that going
any further to the east seems impossible. To the north and south the forest
continues. The ground starts sloping downwards to the west, just after the last
row of trees, down a steep cliff.
name: Dark and Narrow Forest

desc:
Monstrous fir trees loom here, their prickly branches swaying softly against one
another. This in turn causes many black needles to fall down, forming a
pitch-black undergrowth. Some of the branches have lost most of their needles
already, showing only withered, rotten branches. The forest recedes somehow to
the south and to the west, where a small path can be seen.
name: Border of a Tenebrous Forest

desc:
The ground slopes heavily to the west where a steep, tree-covered slope leads
down to a trail. The dark forest here is very dense and its gnarled trees rise
up high, blocking nearly all light with a canopy of interwoven branches. The
forest ground is covered with dark lichen, ferns, and roots that grow among the
many boulders that cover the ground.
name: Sloping Forest

desc:
Black wood can be spotted all around, as many dark fir trees have taken root
here. Thick roots cover most of the undergrowth, seeking to drain as much
nutrition as possible. Thick, withered branches block whatever light might shine
from above, which adds to the already gloomy atmosphere.
name: Tenebrous Forest

desc:
Thick, dark fir trees grow densely together, their many branches intent on
stealing most sunlight from the scarce vegetation below them. Many gnarled roots
protrude from the trees, blocking an easy path through the trees. A multitude of
fir needles lie scattered on the damp ground, colouring the undergrowth mostly
pitch-black.
name: Tenebrous Forest

desc:
Balancing oneself on a few thick, sturdy branches, it is possible to stand
upright on the crown of the tree. Its branches are marked with odd cracks, that
seem unrelated to weight issues, yet as if something was attacking the tree from
inside. A couple of branches grow almost horizontally, yet only southwards do
they meet another tree branch.
name: High up in a Tree

desc:
Upon several thick branches, that have entangled themselves into a platform, it
is possible to see quite far. However, large crowns of the surrounding trees
block most of the sight, limiting the view. A few branches slowly spiral
upwards, higher up in the tree. Crimson-coloured specks dot most of the
branches, the same colour as the leaves growing on the tree.
name: Thick Branches

desc:
From the highest point of the tree, a magnificent sight can be beheld as you can
spot the forest around, thousands of tree tops covering the view below. Several
branches here have been cut, a blood-like substance appears to have dried up,
having dripped to a branch below. Close by, some actual dried up blood is seen,
a large blot covering the grey wood.
name: On Top of a Huge Crown

desc:
There is a massive tree growing here at the edge of Mirkwood. Movement is
hampered here due to the massive roots encircling the trunk. However, there are
lots of strong branches, giving relatively easy passage up into the tree. There
is no way to continue straight to the east or to the north from here.
name: Massive Tree near the Edge of Mirkwood

desc:
Bubbling grey pools of mud encircle a few thick, black trees. Thick vines
surround the trees, coiling around them and ever growing upwards. Only a small
amount of vegetation grows near the muddy pool, most of it being shrivelled. The
corpse of a small creature lies in one of the pools, reeking heavily.
name: Border of the Forest

desc:
The sky above is mostly blocked by dark fir trees, their withered branches
adorned with sharp, black needles. A small, nearly dried out pond can be found
here, partly hidden by the vegetation that is determined to survive. A few
gnarled roots have planted themselves in the pond.
name: Tenebrous Forest

desc:
The surrounding forest gives way to a small copse of red-brown fir trees. The
once smooth bark is now covered with deep lacerations and rotting holes that
have caused most of the needles to abandon the decaying limbs and fall to the
matted forest floor below.
name: Dark Stand of Conifers

desc:
A thick, all-consuming mist moves between the bases of the trees, obscuring the
ground in an ominous haze. Occasionally the mist rises and falls, as if it is
breathing on its own accord. Rotten stumps protrude upwards from the milky mist,
their bark tattered and torn. Thick strands of spider silk prevent the dead bark
from falling to the ground, enveloping the stump in a crown of bark and small,
half-eaten animal corpses.
name: Misty Forest

desc:
Large dark oak trees have interwoven with various beeches in their decrepit
growth. The trees hold the same dark complexion as the surrounding forest. The
limbs thrust upwards into a darkened sky and intermingle into a thick, choking
blanket that suffocates all light attempting to break through.
name: Mixed Forest of Dark Trees

desc:
Vast trees grow together in tight clusters here. Their trunks are covered with a
putrid-smelling ooze that the trees themselves seem to be excreting. Spindly
spider silk hangs from the nearby branches and slowly moves towards any warmth
that comes near it. A great wall of toppled trees, interwoven with thick, thorny
vines prevents travel towards the north.
name: Web-draped Forest

desc:
Enormous beech trees thrust upward from the matted forest floor. A thick layer
of webbing has wound its way around the pasty-coloured trunks, restricting the
growth of the limbs and forcing them into gnarled, twisted masses through which
fingers of thin, rope-like ivy grow. Toward the east lies the source of the
ensnaring ivy, which has coiled and roped itself into an all-consuming mass,
making travel in that direction impossible.
name: Thick Stand of Gnarled Beech

desc:
Large, dark trees close in about a fallen corner of this gloomy forest. Towards
the north and west, a large wall of toppled trees lies in a great pile; a thick
tangle of branches and limbs intertwined with dense, ghostly-white cobwebs
creates an impassable barricade in those directions. A heavy layer of rotten
humus crunches loudly underfoot, and moves slightly when trodden upon.
name: Dark Forested Corner

desc:
Once massive dark trees of mixed variety grew here in great masses, blotting out
the skies above with their twisted canopies and enormous limbs. Now all that
remains are the rotting boughs and stumps that scatter the landscape, making
travel cumbersome in the least. Snaking arms of ivy, armoured with large,
razor-sharp tines grow across the forest floor in a knotted blanket, inviting
death to whomever might linger here long enough to become ensnared.
name: Thick Growth of Ivy

desc:
The trees are of mixed kinds and have dropped many darkened branches on the
ground, that make a silent passing very difficult. The ground holds some
moss-covered rocks and there seems to be no end to the forest around here.
name: Forest at a Ridge

desc:
This grassy plain is the last field before entering the huge and dark forest of
Mirkwood that stands to the southeast. From here, it is impossible to tell how
far the forest stretches, or if it even has an end at all. The ground slopes
slightly to the north where a few flowers grow together. To the west and north,
the landscape consists of grassy fields that cover the plateau.
name: Field next to a Forest

desc:
A valley with a curving trail runs to the west far below the cliff. To the east,
the forest of Mirkwood rises high and mighty. On this field, however, there are
only a couple of smaller trees that grow without a direct connection to the
forest. The stands of lonesome trees grow among the flowers and bushes that mark
the landscape. A loud humming noise comes from the stand of trees.
name: Field above a Valley

desc:
Sloping downwards into a deep dip in the plains, the ground is probably the
bottom of an old lake. However it is not soggier than an ordinary meadow and
thus travelling across it could be done without sinking. Some bear droppings can
be made out along the western edge of the dip.
name: Deep Dip in the Plains

desc:
A blanket of dark greyish undergrowth covers the ground. Some more of the same
undergrowth grows beneath the ash trees slightly taller and straighter, like in
many other places in the northern lands. The gloomy vegetation continues in the
same fashion to the west, while the plains become rockier in the north.
name: Field with a Stand of Ash Trees

desc:
The grassy plains, stretching miles wide to the southwest, end as a corner to
the northeast. The brush has also grown too thick to pass through to the north.
Travelling to the east has been made impossible by a long and high stone row;
however, looking through it, a deep valley running north-south can be seen.
name: Field Ending in a Long Stone Row

desc:
While overlooking the north-south trail that is to the east, an adventurer would
not be visible from down below. This field is part of the expansive plains that
continue for many miles to the north, south and west. A few dark bushes are
growing close to the brink of the escarpment to the east.
name: Field above a Trail

desc:
The sides of a hill are sloping so heavily that riding is impossible and while
there is some grass on the ground, it offers little opportunities for a good
footing. Going south seems impossible, as the slope becomes nearly vertical.
name: Heavily Sloping Plain

desc:
Black water flows down a narrow but deep ditch a couple of yards below, leaving
patches of dark mud on its sides. Some bushes to the east happily drink from the
murky water without any problems. Next to the stream the ground is slightly
damp, but closer to the plateau to the west, the ground is completely dry.
name: Field Drained by a Dark Ditch

desc:
After a heavy slope, the field ends in a kind of hill among the plains. The lack
of protective trees has left the ground vulnerable to the wind and small but
predominantly hardy grasses have grown here because of it. To the south the high
plain is on level ground, but to the east the ground slopes heavily.
name: High Plain

desc:
The ground has dried out and is showing cracks in many places while it slopes
down to the east. Because of the dryness of the earth few plants survive here,
leaving the landscape with a very barren impression. To the north, a little more
grass is growing and in the distant east significantly more. The dark brush and
vegetation can be distinguished easily, standing out in the plains.
name: Dry Plateau

desc:
Webs cover this small tunnel as it disappears into the darkness to the west.
Hundreds of tiny insects have been entombed into the sides of the tunnel within
small cocoons. The passage's top seems to stop at a thick, webbed cover that
must have been made by some enormous spider due to its immense size.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
For this part of Middle-earth, the bushes and plants of the meadow almost give
the impression of a garden. Further to the south, however, the nicely ordered
grasses and bushes become sparser and the grass gradually turns into a greyish
colour as it starts to shrink in size.
name: Garden-like Meadow

desc:
Thin grass, not growing higher than an inch, of a pale grey colour makes the
sloping ground look almost like a worn road paved with cobblestone. The slope in
these craggy plains is very mild, but some abandoned fields can be seen above to
the west with little difficulty.
name: Grey Plains

desc:
The grassy field appears empty and devoid of all creatures, leaving vegetation
to grow freely and unabated. Perhaps it was some predator roaming the area,
scaring away less ferocious animals and finally, without any prey left, moving
on to hunt at other venues, such as the surrounding plains.
name: Grassy Expanse

desc:
Thick brush makes movement considerably harder and the vegetation grows so high
to the north and east that it is not even possible to continue in those
directions. Instead, a field with notable bushes to the west and the extension
of the brushland to the south are the only options for a traveller.
name: Thick Brushland

desc:
Dark brush carpets the ground with shrubs and bushes, leaving little ground for
unhindered movement. Tracks of some of the animals in the area have become
obvious since the high vegetation has been trampled. In the north the brush
becomes larger, while in the west there are some ash trees visible in the
distance.
name: Shrubby Brushland

desc:
The field slopes down heavily towards a brink on the far eastern end of the
plain where a trail can be seen many fathoms below. The ground is steadily
covered with grasses and low-growing vegetation. Luckily, there is no real risk
of falling over and rolling off the edge of the cliff.
name: Steep Hillside

desc:
As if constantly being fried by the sun, the vegetation on the field is faded
and the ground is very dry. Distinct cracks on the ground show where any rain
water quickly would drain away. To the south, the ground slopes upwards until
some miles in the distance a small hill top can be sighted.
name: Dried-out Plain

desc:
Lemon grass and other kinds of grasses that flourish in the very damp soil,
encircle the small pond in the plains. Plentiful bird droppings on the ground
leave a greeting from the many birds that come here and the surrounding plains
continue in all directons.
name: Pond in Some Wet Plains

desc:
Low brush covers most of the area, giving protection for a variety of insects
and smaller animals. In the middle, however, the ground has lower vegetation in
a circular area, as if a long lost building once stood there. Plains stretch out
in all directions, with the southern ones sloping up in a steep fashion.
name: Circle of Brush

desc:
From the highest point among the fields, nothing but more grassy plains can be
seen stretching in the horizon to the northeast. A little trail runs from the
north to the south, far below to the east, but from here it is impossible to
tell whether someone is travelling along it. Since there are no trees in the
area, laying down here in the grass would be perfect for studying the sky.
name: Atop a Hill

desc:
The grass-covered ground slopes upwards to the west, leading to the highest
point in the area. To the south, however, easy travelling is made impossible
when the field ends above an escarpment. Following the field past its northern
end leads downward to a wide sea of high grasses.
name: Sloping Plain

desc:
The surroundings are dark and the trees numerous here although it is close to
the edge of the forest. To the north, the forest is changing into the plains and
fields. The grass has turned from green to some sick-looking black colour and
the rocks on the ground are completely infested with dark lichen. While the
distant valley holding a trail can barely be seen to the west, it is impossible
to tell how far the mighty forest stretches to the south and east.
name: Edge of Mirkwood

desc:
In the dense forest it is impossible to see very far in any direction. The
darkness of the trees casts eerie shadows across the root-covered and fern-
covered forest floor. It is possible to walk between the trees and follow the
mossy undergrowth in all directions but the south, where a significantly larger
effort would be needed to push south through some dark, dense bushes.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
A monstrous tree has been uprooted, its trunk forming a roof overhead. Beneath
the trunk, a myriad of twisting roots twirl about, seeking to snare the life
force out of whatever they encounter. Under the tree, the undergrowth is thick
with many needled leaves and every here and there a few oak leaves and small,
twisted twigs.
name: Under Some Massive Roots

desc:
Great blackened trees with coarse grey bark tower high into the sky above. The
thick bark covering the trees has been contorted into odd patterns; some
resembling demonic faces, others depicting a horror-filled snapshot of some
unsuspecting victim's last living moments. Whatever evil has permeated these
woodlands seems bent on making its malice known. A massive uprooting lies to the
west where a great tree has fallen, creating an insurmountable thicket of roots
and mulch.
name: Forest of Disfigured Faces

desc:
The forest has parted a little here, allowing for slightly easier passage
through some brush. Scattered among the undergrowth are several skeletons; some
have elongated spines attached to large skulls, and others bear the features of
humanoids. A half-decomposed arm, with a bony hand still attached, clutches at a
rusty pike that lies half-buried in the decaying matter that decomposes on the
forest floor.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
The forest here grows stunted and black. The limbs have grown out only a short
way from the trunks, and have formed into a tangle of twisted branches and
twigs. The bark of the surrounding trees is of a deep black-green colour and is
covered with dreadful cracks which ooze a strange, smelly liquid. Several
gnarled pine trees, intermixed with dark firs, grow densely towards the west,
where several of their massive trunks intertwine with the branches of the pines
and create an impassable forest wall.
name: Blackened Forest

desc:
Large, towering oak trees thrust upward from a rotten blanket of fallen foliage.
Blood and carnage almost completely cover the lower base of the trees, while
small pieces and gobs of flesh are scattered about. The canopy above grows
tightly together, casting a deep gloom over the forest floor below whilst trying
to strangle any stray ray of light before it reaches the ground. Toward the west
a dense, impenetrable mass of writhing roots coalesces around a thick growth of
dark fir trees, completely obstructing passage in that direction.
name: Beneath Blood-Spattered Oaks

desc:
Enormous black oak trees tower high above, before fading into an ominous haze
that engulfs the canopy and swallows all form of twig and branch alike. These
black sentinels keep silent watch over all who travel below. Thick, dark bark
resembles their armour, enormous branches their arms and weapons.
name: Copse of Black Trees

desc:
The trees here grow in thick clusters, reaching upwards together towards the sky
above. At various points, the branches have grown together with their neighbours
before protruding out into any available space. A dark layer of green moss grows
within the nooks and crannies formed by the struggling tree limbs, which now lie
motionless as if frozen in time before the victor could be decided.
name: Dark Stand of Trees

desc:
Great, twisted tree trunks have grown together into a tangled, writhing mass.
Large cracks have splintered down the thin, sickly bark, revealing deep oozing
wounds. Thin branches sprout out from the base of the trees and have grown into
the shape of gnarled claw-like talons, at the ends of which drip a putrid,
yellow substance that emits an abhorrent stench.
name: Among Sickly Trees

desc:
A thick growth of sickly beech trees stands here in silent disarray. A dark evil
continues to warp these trees into a twisted mass of pale-barked, spindly-limbed
abominations. Through deep cracks in the bark, a thick, oozing sap is secreted,
coating the blackened foliage with a sickly film.
name: Stand of Sickly Trees

desc:
Here a large number of dank fir trees have grown amongst the vast number of dark
oaks. Their limbs are thin and spindly, and are covered with a brownish-red,
bubbling fungus that produces a rancid smell. Small, blood-red berries cling to
the ends of the branches in small clusters, the surface of each fruit is
disfigured by open slits from which slimy spore sacks are excreted.
name: Stand of Emaciated Trees

desc:
The trees here are covered in a pale, sickly bark on which grows a thin layer of
green mould. From the pallid trunks extend emaciated boughs of crooked,
contorted branches from which malformed foliage sprouts. It is as if some evil
lives under the rotten ground, and has allowed the roots of the trees to drink
in its sickness. Towards the north and south the trees lie in a twisted mass,
restricting any movement in those directions. Upon the ground, there is a patch
of rotten leaves, amongst which a few small, pale green tufts of grass have
grown.
name: Sickening Woodlands

desc:
Dead and broken trees lie toppled and rotting, scattered upon the forest floor.
The ground is covered with a thick layer of variegated humus. A closer
inspection reveals not only decomposing leaves, twigs and branches, but small
decaying animal bones and patches of tattered, bloody fur. A malodorous smell
rises up from the rot and decay of the forest floor and seems to thicken the
surrounding air.
name: Decaying Forest

desc:
Towards the east and west grow massive walls of dark beech which loom tall,
obscuring the sky in a gloomy haze of dangling spider webs and clustered
foliage. The dense forest walls meet towards the south, where a thick hedge of
tangled roots and fallen boughs intertwine into an impassable growth of twisted
forest. The only chance of escaping this suffocating wood lies to the north,
where a carpet of ground-dwelling ivy spreads across the ground in a writhing
mass.
name: Constricted Forest

desc:
Standing tall and wide, this forest is shrouded in malice. A gathering gloom
pervades the space between the leaves, branches and even the filth and decay on
the forest floor. The trees are covered in a thick armour of black, furrowed
bark. To the west, the gloomy forest continues its span into darkness.
name: Darkened Forest

desc:
Far above, a thick canopy of tangled leaves and branches intertwines into a mass
of leaves and spindly spider silk. Small, decaying corpses that once may have
resembled birds or squirrels have been caught in the sticky webbing; some are
partially cocooned, while others have simply been left to rot. A thick tangle of
thorny brambles grows to the south and west, cutting off any attempt at travel
in those directions.
name: Darkened Forest

desc:
Blackened oak trees loom over the forest floor here, their bark seemingly
darkened by the gloom that hangs about. The limbs of the oak trees themselves
appear to be twisted in torment as they interweave with each other.
name: Black Oaks

desc:
The trees here are dark and menacing as they grow on the uneven ground. There
are rises and falls in the ground and the damp foliage on the forest floor grows
thicker as the debris collects on the lower elevations.
name: Uneven Terrain

desc:
The ground here starts a steep incline from the west to the east. The dark
forest has an eerie gloom to it as it thins toward the east on its approach to a
steep cliff. The foliage above is dense enough to block any attempt to discern
the sky beyond this forest.
name: Before a Steep Escarpment

desc:
Warped thorns and brambles are scattered about here on the forest floor. Dark,
broken twigs lie all about the base of the black, twisted trees which grow here.
To the west, through the trees can be seen a drop off as the forest gives way to
a cliff.
name: Overlooking a Cliff Face

desc:
A deep, forested ravine cuts sharply into the earth and continues to slope
downwards towards the east. Various trees grow out from the steep walls of the
valley, struggling for purchase as massive roots rear up, before thrusting back
deep into the earth. Thin strands of sticky spider webbing hang down from thick
branches, waiting to ensnare anything that might get too close. A gathering
gloom spreads from the depths, casting ominous shadows everywhere.
name: Sloping Forest

desc:
In all directions the gloomy woods of Mirkwood spread abroad, save to the south
where a thick growth of shrubs and ivy interweave into an impassable wall of
tangled limbs and thorns. A dreary mist encircles the trees, rising and falling
as it creeps across the forest floor. It engulfs every nook and cranny, leaving
a thin film of foetid moisture over everything it touches. A reddish-brown layer
of moss covers the north face of each tree.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
Under a thick and gloomy canopy of gnarled boughs and branches grow the wide,
dark-coloured trunks that support the dreadful ceiling above. Knotting tightly
together, there is little room for anything else to take root and grow between
these massive boles. Upon the ground lies a thick layer of rotting foliage,
