where a few small, jagged rocks can be seen thrusting up into the suffocating
gloom that lingers heavily between the trees.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
To the north and south, the steep walls of this valley loom high above, stopping
even the most skilled of climbers from navigating them. The ground is littered
with rocks, fallen boughs and boulders. It seems they have lost the battle with
gravity and the elements and tumbled down the sides of the valley and now lie
broken and scattered. The bones of those that were unable to move fast enough
lie strewn about the ground.
name: Gloomy, Forested Valley

desc:
Growing large and in close proximity of one another, the surrounding forest
makes it hard to navigate. The air between the rough boughs and branches is
musty, and settles heavily on anything it lingers upon. Tall and dark, and in
massive quantities lie a growth of gnarled beech towards the north. South and
eastward however yields a thick wall of fallen trees, their great blackened
trunks lying in various piles and directions, and their enormous root systems
exposed in hideous, twisted masses making travel between them quite difficult
for even the most adapted of woodland travellers.
name: Densely Forested Corner

desc:
Here the dark, penetrating gloom of this forest spreads out in all directions.
The forest floor is covered in a thick blanket of broken twigs, leaves, and the
occasional bone fragment. Massive roots thrust up from the earth in various
places, and have slowly intertwined with each other to form a motionless,
brooding monument of root and stem.
name: Deep within a Dark Forest

desc:
Dark mushrooms litter the ground here giving a slight iridescent glow as their
poisonous spores are expelled into the air. Thick shrubs and ivy interweave to
the north, causing a mass of branches which do not allow passage. The tangle of
vegetation continues to the west, with the trunks of beech trees joining in the
natural wall.
name: Corner of a Dark Forest

desc:
Two enormous trees that once may have resembled the lavish mellyrn of Lothlorien
now lie twisted and corrupt. What once were proud, sprawling tree branches, with
golden-yellow leaves and bright blooming flowers now hold no such splendour;
drooping branches, covered with coarse, knotted bark hang in dead disarray. The
once brightly coloured flowers have shrivelled into oozing clusters of small,
black efflorescent spore sacks.
name: Towering Trees

desc:
Great dark trees thrust upward from the thick layer of rotting humus that
blankets the ground. Swaying spider webs hang from the lofty branches above in
great veils of ghostly white silk. An oppressive gloom hangs heavy under the
twisted tangle of limbs that forms the canopy above.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
The trees begin to slouch to the south as the elevation rises. There are thin
spider webs crossing the small trees which grow on this slope. Small animal
holes litter the ground on this rise, threatening to trip travellers at their
every step and sending them onto the sharp limbs and rocks strewn across the
forest floor.
name: Rising Forest

desc:
The trees grow thinly at the top of this rocky rise, and instead the ground is
covered by dense, tangled bushes. The rise overlooks the forest, yet nothing can
be seen of the ground below as the foliage blocks all attempts to discern what
Mirkwood hides beneath its leafy ceiling. There is a faint, eerie fog swirling
across the ground, forming vague shapes then dissipating again, as if made up of
dispersed, fading spirits.
name: Tree-covered Rise

desc:
The forest here emanates gloom and despair. There is a noxious stink that hangs
in the air. To the south can be seen a glimpse of a trail that travels west to
east, but it cannot be reached here. To the west, there is a steep rise up to a
hilltop that cannot be seen due to the mist and thick trees which grow in this
area.
name: Deep in Mirkwood

desc:
The trees coalesce into a corridor with a narrow opening to the north. The
corridor of darkened trees blocks the way to the south. A smelly mist drifts in
from the west. The trees begin to thin out, yet hunch over somewhat to the east.
name: Narrow Woodlands

desc:
The forest floor is covered in a gloomy mist here which makes it hard to see
very far. The ground slopes slightly to the southwest. The trees make a natural
wall to the north which makes travel in this direction impossible.
name: Slightly Sloping Forest

desc:
The gloomy forest becomes thin here and in the middle lies a huge blackened
stump. Strange looking insects move all about it, giving it an appearance of
infestation. A crack is visible at the top of the stump. The forest thickens to
the west and east. The trees begin to form a narrow pass going south.
name: At a Dark Stump

desc:
A dank, musty, hollowed out stump is here surrounded by moss and rotting wood.
The wood and roots of the stump give way to earthen walls in some places. Fungus
covers some of the root-lined walls of this empty stump. To the east, a tunnel
can be seen, dug through the hard packed earth and bits of root growing there.
name: Under the Stump

desc:
Fungi grows everywhere in this festering cave. Sorts and varieties of black and
sickly-looking mould and fungi grow in patches across the walls. There are
patches of dirt that are covered by slime which seems to have been secreted from
the fungi. A lingering, malign aura pollutes the air in the cave as if terrible
crimes were committed here and the evil still lingers.
name: Nasty Cave

desc:
Fire-cracked rock lying in a ruined pattern that resembles what once must have
been a hearth, sits collecting moss here. Small trees, interspersed among the
trunks of the larger ones, grow in an almost diabolic fashion as they
criss-cross to cause a natural fence to the west blocking any passage.
name: Ravaged Hearth

desc:
Two great trees form a natural arch right before the ground begins to slope
sharply downwards to the north, where a misty haze shrouds the depths of the
valley in an ominous gloom. Above, where the dank foliage of the surrounding
trees looms high overhead, great strands of silky white spider webbing hangs
down nearly to the forest floor. Various insects and small animals can be seen
wrapped tightly amongst the webs, indicating the eight-legged architects have
not yet abandoned these feeding grounds.
name: Before a Gloomy Valley

desc:
The constricting walls of the valley begin to open up as the gully weaves its
way north. The light is dull as the gloomy canopy of the great, blackened forest
above grows in closer together, shedding a dreary gloom over the valley floor
far below. Toward the south, the ground begins to ascend a hazardous slope
covered with fallen debris and strewn boulders.
name: Narrow Turn in a Valley

desc:
An enormous pile of fallen debris has settled into the narrow gully at the floor
of the valley, evidence of past landslides. Small pebbles, followed by larger
stones begin to plummet down the steep sides of the valley as the ground begins
to shake.
name: Blocked Gully

desc:
A steep slope in the forest brings the elevation down toward the east. Twisted
limbs reach out at head level threatening to scrape the face. The trees here
grow in leaning positions so as to compensate for the slope but it causes a bent
over, decrepit look to them.
name: Slope in the Gloomy Forest

desc:
A thick matting of leaves and forest debris lines the ground here allowing many
different types of fungi to grow. The black-barked trees loom high overhead
giving an ambience of despair. Of the sky above, there is not even a sliver
visible from beneath the dark canopy.
name: In a Depression in Mirkwood

desc:
A tunnel extends from west to east, nearly eight feet high and widest in the
middle where it almost forms a small cavern. Its dark, earthy walls are uneven
and look to have been brutishly carved out by giant hands. Broken roots protrude
from the ceiling, walls, and even the floor in some places, crudely cleared from
the tunnel's path. The ground at the southern edge of the passage is
particularly disturbed, where there seems to be another opening.
name: Widening Troll Burrow

desc:
A crude cavern has been carved out of hard, black soil. The cave is at least
twenty feet in height and easily three times that in width. The chamber seems to
be located under a forest as many roots break through the soil that make up the
ceiling. There are signs of inhabitation on the floor, mainly piles of leaves
and other vegetation that might serve as simple bedding.
name: Troll Home

desc:
One end of a tunnel abruptly stops here, narrowing and sloping sharply upwards.
Rough earthen ledges of packed dirt provide purchase to anyone moving from the
tunnel towards the forest floor above. The other end of the passage extends
underground to the east, where the humid scent of damp soil almost overwhelms
the ever-present trollish odour.
name: Narrow, Gouged Passage

desc:
The rough trail underfoot turns southward, skirting a jagged, rocky slope toward
the east and continuing deeper into the suffocating darkness between gnarled,
corrupted trees. The forest mixes with dense shrubs to the north.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
A thin, silky layer of cobwebs lines the inner layer of the canopy as it stoops
above the trail which travels in an east and west direction. The trees blot out
the sky here forcing a gloom which hangs in the air.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
A looming canopy of trees covers the trail on the northern and southern sides
here as the murky path continues in a dark, tunnel-like fashion. There is an
eerie fog which hangs in the air here, making all noises in this area
unsettlingly muffled.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
The looming black trees thin just enough to allow a rough trail to wind its way
into the gloom in the east and the west. Their contorted trunks and crooked
limbs are adorned with thick, black vines bristling with barbed thorns. To the
north, these trees press in close, blocking passage in that direction
completely. Passage between the trees to the south looks possible, but
difficult. The air is malevolently silent and suffocatingly still.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
The trail continues to weave its way east between the twisted, disfigured trees,
whose thick, web-encrusted canopy above chokes out even the smallest sliver of
sky. Black, wickedly-thorned vines have wound themselves around some of the
branches as if in an attempt to smother what perverted life still exists in
them.
name: Bend in the Trail of Shadows

desc:
An oppressive gloom pervades the space between the surrounding walls of the
thickening forest that line this trail on all sides, save to the south where the
track continues its way through dark, twisting trees. Overhead, the grasping
branches come together to form a ceiling of gnarled, twisted limbs.
Ghostly-white blankets of silky spider webs hang down from above, nearly
touching the ground in some places.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
Trees of mixed varieties grow densely here; some thin, spindly fir trees grow
amongst other much wider and hardier oak and beech, and the occasional ash tree.
The ground is covered in a mottled blanket of orange fir needles and brown oak
and beech leaves, all of which are partially coated in unusual spots of black
slime that slowly devours anything it touches.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
Several skeletons, most of them partly decayed, litter the path that twists
through the brush. Most of the skeletons are of animals, big and small, that
wandered through the area, yet every here and there the remains of a humanoid
can be seen, a rusted axe or sword piercing them.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
A heavily overgrown path twists and curves around in the brush, with rotten tree
stumps and thick undergrowth, thorny brambles, and gnarled, upturned roots
creating a maze. A few trees still remain with their dark leafy crowns, but not
enough to blocking light from sun and moon. Thick, thorny vines ensnare a few
dark trees southwards, creating cracks in the bark.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
Several small mounds of dirt lie alongside the path, which is mostly covered
with long, black, and mottled sickle-shaped leaves. Therefore, the path itself
is difficult to follow, luring those that travel upon it northwards into the
forest. A fallen oak tree, its bark rotten and crawling with a multitude of
insects, lies eastwards.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
The path goes here meandering through a dense brushes, making the path more
difficult to follow. It's still possible to ride here. The route to the west and
south is impassable due to some dense brushes. The path continues to the north,
but someone has built a barricade made mostly of wood and some metal parts on
the path. Lots of boulders and wood pillars have been installed on the sides of
the barricade up to the impassable dense brushes, making it impossible to go
around the barricade.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
Between the thick, pitch-black trees that thrive here, hang thick cobwebs, their
silvery shine in sharp contrast with the flora. In a few places, the webs have
been cut, the strands hanging down from the branches. In them, insects nearly a
foot tall can be spotted, trapped until hunger strikes the owner of the web.
name: Amongst Thick Cobwebs

desc:
Artificially enlarged, this burrow now provides enough room to allow several
humanoids to occupy it. Several cracks inhibit the earth above, although
somewhat hidden by the wooden support beams. A small pile of trash litters the
southern wall of the burrow, the only distinguishable items being bones of
various sizes. A hole has been half-dug beside the pile, only a foot deep so
far.
name: Hidden Burrow

desc:
Sharp spikes of varying lengths have been strategically placed on the earthen
floor, leaving almost no room for one to walk safely. The spikes are not the
only objects to be found here, as a few carcasses in different decaying states
and rusted armour and weapons cover them. This tunnel is quite deep underground,
as the entrance from above is too high for even the largest humanoid to reach.
The walls are slippery, a slimy and watery substance dripping from every side.
name: Spike Trap

desc:
Dark, thorny vines slowly choke the vegetation found here, encircling tree and
bushes alike. The vines also cover the ground, its sharp, black thorns menacing
unprotected limbs. The few plants that once thrived in these surroundings, have
all shrivelled up and turned a menacing black. A few corpses of small fauna,
decayed into little more than skeletons, remain trapped in the vines.
name: Dense Thicket

desc:
Many small trees grow here, not yet tall or thick enough to truly be part of the
forest. They are heavily surrounded by thick brush, and a few trees have fallen
victim to dark green, thorny smothering vines. A clearing can be found in the
southern quarter, where twigs and leaves of tree, bush, and plant alike have
been eaten by wildlife.
name: Small Clearing

desc:
Looming from high above, thick, gnarled tree trunks blot out most of the
sunlight above, their thick leaves and pine needles littering the undergrowth.
Where the sunlight managed to shine through, the brushwood has grown large and
lush. Scattered through the undergrowth, lie hundreds of black insect corpses,
emitting a foul odour.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Tall and dark trees, most of them resembling oak and beech, have firmly planted
their roots in the soil here. A great deal of these trees present bare holes of
over seven inches wide, grey slime dripping from them. The same slime has
created a trail over the undergrowth, bark, and nearby vegetation.
name: Under Some Mutilated Trees

desc:
Besides the tall, gnarled trees that loom here, no other vegetation can be
found. The trees which, usually are blacker than pitch, now bear grey to light
grey marks upon them. No more leaves adorn the trees, allowing light from above
to illuminate this area. Ashes and a few humanoid skeletons cover the ground,
hinting of a fire not so long ago.
name: Charred Trees

desc:
Towering dark trees, which might one day have been beech, oak, or fir trees,
bear gnarled branches with sometimes black needles or mottled palmate leaves.
The branches and needles are speckled with crimson coloured dots. Some trees
suffer from cracked bark, a greyish ooze dripping slowly from the cracks.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Thick, pitch-black trees grow closely together here, their upturned roots and
branches intertwining with each other, blocking off obvious paths to the north
and west. Between the trees, hot, bubbling piles of mud have formed, reeking
heavily. To the south, the forest becomes less dense, as many trees that would
normally have blocked the sight have been reduced to stumps.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Only a small number of trees, most of them nothing more than growing stems,
still grow here, as their brethren have been felled. Thick dark tree stumps
cover the entire area, some of them already overgrown with new vegetation. Every
here and there, a rusted axe remains rooted in a stump.
name: Decimated Forest

desc:
Hundreds of ferns grow tall here, their many leaves blocking the view
everywhere, except above. The ferns are oddly coloured, mostly black and grey,
every here and there also hinting of magenta. There are a few small openings,
where most ferns have shrivelled completely, covered by dark green, thorny
plants.
name: Between Black Ferns

desc:
Small, sharp rocks cover the ground, several of them crushing some of the
vegetation. Large bramble bushes bearing sharp, thorny branches dominate the
view, although other bushes are not too uncommon. Lots of berries adorn the
undergrowth, some of them colouring the rocks almost completely dark purple. A
few gnarled roots can be seen in the northern part of this area, part of the
dark forest close by.
name: Thorny Bushes

desc:
A myriad of different thick bushes have taken root here. Not one of them
resembles the other in terms of length, width, fruit or leaves, yet nearly all
of them bear the same large and sharp thorns, over two inches wide, grey to
black in colour and bearing red dots. Several thorns litter the ground, their
prickly ends a menace to body parts unprotected by good armour.
name: Diseased Brush

desc:
a horde of black insects crawl on the undergrowth, infesting plants and bushes
alike. A few bushes have been entirely destroyed by them, leaving only a few
bare branches. A lone gnarled tree, covered in crimson dots and bearing several
cracks in its bark, remains unperturbed.
name: Infested Brush

desc:
Growing closely together, great pitch-black trees loom here, their thick, leafy
branches swaying softly, barring any light coming from above. Black, thorny
vines, have coiled themselves around a few trees, cracking the bark. Below lies
only vegetation, pale in colour, stunted and gnarled, most being nothing more
than weeds.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The forest grows quite dense, tree trunks being no further apart than three
feet, their roots and branches curving around each other. The thick, black weeds
twist between the trees ensnaring and consuming any other flora that try to take
root. Some yellow mushrooms hang from the trees, leeching away the sap and
exuding a foul odour.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The trail runs north and east. The ground gradually becomes moist and more
treacherous along the southeastern edge. The trees have thinned enough to allow
one to peer through them to the south, yet not allowing one to travel through
them.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
The trail travels from the west where it is marshy and soft and turns compact
and hard toward the east as the trail narrows. The way north is partly blocked
by thick trunks of trees and vegetation but a narrow passageway can probably be
found. To the south, a small path leads away from the trail and into the trees.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
Inside the cave, rows of sharpened poles form a partial barricade against those
who wish to force their entry into this orkish outpost. Behind the crude
fortifications, the camp is in disarray. A couple of crude benches stand in the
centre of the room, around which scattered tools, bones, and detritus from
expanding the cave make for treacherous footing, particularly as the dirt floor
gives way to uneven rock. Several filthy banners hang from metal pegs in the
greyish cave wall, proclaiming the glory of the Morgundul.
name: Outpost under the Hills

desc:
This small, rough-walled chamber is separated with uncharacteristic privacy from
the room to the west by a hanging wolfskin, and houses several rows of
uncomfortable-looking bedding. The smell of orc is inescapable in this cramped
living space, and despite the organised layout of the room the rough stone floor
is a mess of rotten food, discarded clothes, and malodourous stains.
name: Sleeping Quarters

desc:
Twenty feet across, this orkish armory is roughly square, with an open passage
towards the cave entrance to the south and an iron grate on the eastern wall.
The walls are surprisingly smooth, suggesting that this room was carved out with
some care. Racks of vicious-looking weapons lie against the western wall of the
room, and a shoddy stack of supply crates fills one corner. In the centre of the
room is a broad wooden table, askew with equipment in varying states of repair
and carved with a crude sketch of the surrounding area.
name: Outpost under the Hills

desc:
A small, dingy room has been carved here, more of a recess in the cave wall than
a proper chamber. The ground is a mat of rotten, stinking straw, a thin mockery
of comfort. There are no windows, and the rough-hewn walls show no decoration
save the hopeless scratching of one desperate former resident. The room is
sealed off from the rest of the outpost by an iron grate, which hangs heavy but
ill-fitting on its rusted hinges.
name: Filthy Cave

desc:
Dark, looming trees tower overhead on either side of the trail, giving the
impression of a tunnel. The way north and south is completely blocked by the
huge trunks of the black-barked trees. The trail itself is little more than a
narrow path of trampled soil, mostly free of undergrowth.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
The dark trees loom over the trail, keeping it in eternal shadow. The trees are
mostly old, gnarly oaks with sprawling branches and dark leaves. To the north,
the trees grow so densely that they form an impassable wall. The shadowy trail
continues to the east and west.
name: The Trail of Shadows

desc:
Thick, short oak trees grow here in a twisted mass. The tops of the trees just
overhead grow together, mutating into one mass of branches. The thick oak trees
have been blackened at their trunks by the gloom that hangs over this forest.
name: Thick Patch of Black Oaks

desc:
Wooden and metal spikes are set in the ground, although the dried blood that
covers them makes the distinction hard. They are razor-sharp and with most of
the metal ones having varying degrees of rust, provide a painful, if not lethal,
experience for those that might land upon them. A skeleton or two are pinned on
the spikes already, some still bearing rotting flesh.
name: Spike Trap

desc:
Several pine trees have taken root here, interspersed with dark fir trees. The
crowns of the trees looming above bear thick, crooked branches that effectively
block out all light coming from above. Many branches lie scattered on the
undergrowth, preventing easy movement through the forest.
name: Tenebrous Forest

desc:
Thick, silvery cobwebs have been spun between the monstrous, black trees that
thrive here. Thick, gnarled branches that bear speckled needles have thrust
themselves through some of the webs, leaving only a few strands hanging on the
branch itself. A few cocoons, about a feet wide, adorn the webs.
name: Webbed, Dark Forest

desc:
Many crates and pegs fill up this small room. Many of them are filled with
various weapons, although some appear to be broken and beyond use. No windows
are to be found, the only visible exit lies to the north, where an oaken door
can be seen. The room is quite musty, hinting it may not be visited often.
name: Musty Armoury

desc:
Cold water slowly flows through a breach in the cavern wall, into a wave-less
lake that fills the huge, domed cave. A slow trickle of water can be heard as
the water pours in, and then trickles out through a tiny crevice in the eastern
wall. A dim, yellow glow can be seen coming from the mossy shore to the north.
name: Cavern Lake

desc:
The cold water plunges around several chaotic bends, as it curves through the
darkness along the underground caves. The blackened, limestone walls have been
worn smooth by the running water, but scrape painfully upon contact. The force
of the water slowly quells as it reaches a larger, solemn cave in the east.
name: Underground Stream

desc:
A few stalactites hang on the cavern ceiling, each over a few feet long. Drops
of water fall down from them, resulting in an ever present dripping sound and
ripples forming in the dark lake. A few inches of stalagmite break the surface
of the lake. Dark grey and green algae cover the lake, hiding its contents even
more. The lake occupies the entire cavern, leaving little space for dry feet.
name: Underground Lake

desc:
Several fir planks have been nailed into the crown of this sturdy oak providing
a place of rest. The position of this platform has been carefully thought out
and hidden due to the heavy foliage. A clever hatch in the floor provides access
back down the tree but a sloping branch to the east seems to serve a similar
purpose.
name: Platform in the Trees

desc:
The forest to the southeast does not entirely reach to the edge of this plateau
above the valley. Instead a brushland, with the same kind of thick undergrowth
as within the forest, spreads towards the edge. Parts of the valley and the
trail following it below can be watched from here, but going down is impossible
because of the steep slope.
name: Dense Brush above a Valley
